untranslatable_reason: Signal requires external data not available in standard OHLCV indicators: (1) Inventory levels and percentile ranges (2-year historical inventory data), (2) Production pipeline lead times in days, (3) Demand volatility coefficient of variation calculated from demand data, (4) Storage costs as percentage of commodity price. These are fundamental/supply-chain variables that cannot be derived from price/volume/technical indicators alone. The signal is economically sound but requires a dedicated commodity data feed with inventory, production, and cost-of-carry metrics.
translation_notes: MICROSTRUCTURE_DRY_20_2 is a supply-chain arbitrage signal exploiting inventory mismatches and storage cost dynamics. While all entry/exit timing conditions can be specified (50th percentile threshold, 30-day pipeline lag, CV>0.25, storage>5% of price), the required input variables are not available in the allowed indicator set. Implementation would require: (a) External inventory database integration, (b) Production schedule data, (c) Commodity-specific cost-of-carry calculations. The signal structure is valid but data sources are outside technical analysis scope.
